human_review_reason: The source content is an introductory excerpt only; the substantive policy arguments, stance, and conclusions are not available. The speech_neutral classification is speculative based on limited text. Full PDF should be retrieved before final classification and archival acceptance.

corrected_fields.summary: Bank of France Governor François Villeroy de Galhau delivered an introductory portion of a speech at Sciences Po on April 2, 2026, discussing whether recent geopolitical events (war in the Middle East) might replicate the 2022 energy price shock. The available excerpt describes the start of 2026 monetary policy as being in a favorable "2+2" configuration (2% inflation, 2% interest rate). The Governor begins to address why we are not witnessing a repeat of 2022. Note: this is only the introductory portion (~398 words) of a 15-page speech; substantive policy arguments are not included in this excerpt.
corrected_fields.why_it_matters: As a G7 central bank governor, Villeroy's assessment of whether current geopolitical developments will replicate 2022's inflationary shock could be significant for market expectations around ECB policy trajectory. However, the available excerpt only contains introductory framing; the substantive arguments and policy conclusions are not accessible in this truncated version. This record should be updated once the full 15-page PDF is retrieved.
corrected_fields.macro_context: The available excerpt directly addresses macro catalyst risks - specifically whether an energy price shock from Middle East conflict could reignite inflation similar to 2022. Villeroy begins to argue 2022 is not repeating, but the full argument is not included. The '2+2' framing describes a favorable starting position at the beginning of 2026.
